personal.name: Nikita Kazeev
personal.title: Research Scientist at the intersection of AI and Physics

note.title: h-index
note.body: And from the height of my [citation pile](https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=vamy2okAAAAJ), I'm telling you: don't use h-index to judge people

highlights.title: AI Strategy & Platform Architecture
bullets: Advised on AI/ML strategy, product implementation, and technical design for the platform.

highlights.title: Leadership
bullets: Speaker search and selection for the 500-person AI4X 2025 conference.
Main organizer of the ICLR 2025 workshop on multiscale machine learning.

highlights.title: Transformer for generating symmetric crystals
bullets: Created a generative model for material design based on symmetry inductive bias.
Implemented the model in PyTorch.
Achieved the best generation quality and diversity among space-group-conditioned models.
Led a team of 6.

highlights.title: ML for predicting properties of defects in 2D crystals
bullets: Created a sparse representation of crystals with defects, achieving 4x energy prediction error reduction.
Developed code for reproducible parallel running of machine learning experiments.
Led a team of 3.

highlights.title: AI/ML Consulting
bullets: Consulted on machine learning algorithms, deep learning applications, and architectural designs for scientific and industry-led projects.

workExperience.note: [2025 Breakthrough Prize in Fundamental Physics](https://breakthroughprize.org/Laureates/1/L3995) as a member of LHCb.

highlights.title: Generative models uncertainty estimation
bullets: Developed the first methods for estimating uncertainty of conditional GANs.
Led a team of 3 students.

highlights.title: Generative models for fast simulation
bullets: Developed a GAN-based machine learning model for high-fidelity simulation of a Cherenkov detector trained on tabular data.
Achieved approximately 10^5 speed-up compared to the ab-initio simulator.

highlights.title: Machine learning on noisy data
bullets: Developed a rigorous way to train ML algorithms on data with the label-noise model common in high-energy physics.

highlights.title: Muon identification at the LHCb experiment at CERN
bullets: Solved a classification problem over tabular data with noisy labels under timing constraints.
Developed a gradient boosting model that reduced the false positive rate by 30% in the critical low-track-momentum region.
Integrated the model into LHCb production, mostly in C++.
Packaged the work as a data science competition problem for IDAO-2019.

highlights.title: CatBoost aka fighting biases with dynamic boosting
bullets: Set up a distributed system for running experiments for the team with Bash and Python.
Studied gradient boosting improvements with experiments on toy data.

education.title: Higher School of Economics (HSE University)
education.body: PhD in Computer Science, supervisor [Andrey Ustyuzhanin](https://www.linkedin.com/profile/view?id=2422149); [Machine Learning for particle identification in the LHCb detector](https://repository.cern/records/034yx-nt191).

education.title: Sapienza — Universita di Roma
education.body: PhD in Physics (double degree with HSE), supervisor [Barbara Sciascia](https://it.linkedin.com/in/barbara-sciascia-234935a).

education.title: Product Management course at Yandex
education.body: Focused coursework in product strategy and execution.

education.title: Yandex School of Data Analysis
education.body: Master's level CS course covering algorithms, machine learning, deep learning, and distributed systems.

education.title: Moscow Institute of Physics and Technology
education.body:
**MS in Physics** (2014–2016): Optimisation of data processing of the LHCb experiment.

**BS in Physics** (2010–2014): Study of the quantum states of the electrons in nonideal plasma and selected molecules using wave packet molecular dynamics with packet splitting.

mentorship.body: Mentored 9 students ([1](https://www.hse.ru/edu/vkr/219430466), [2](https://www.hse.ru/edu/vkr/296294066), [3](https://www.hse.ru/edu/vkr/219524130), [4](https://www.hse.ru/en/edu/vkr/470561417), [5](https://www.hse.ru/en/edu/vkr/470935224), [6](https://www.hse.ru/ma/datasci/students/diplomas/631565328), [7](https://www.hse.ru/ma/datasci/students/diplomas/631565422), [8](https://www.hse.ru/edu/vkr/624890096), [9](https://www.hse.ru/ba/ami/students/diplomas/834351454)), 3 interns, and student workshop projects ([1](https://sochisirius.ru/news/3148), [2](https://web.archive.org/web/20220512040654/https://academy.yandex.ru/posts/vossozdat-arkhitekturu-bazy-dannykh-i-nauchit-golosovogo-pomoschnika-predskazyvat-namereniya-polzovatelya)).
Student council member from 2013 to 2016, leading several dormitory-scale IT projects.
Co-PI of a $3.4m AI Singapore grant on multiscale machine learning.

teachingAndOutreach.body: Pitch research to the general public through major newspaper coverage ([1](https://www.kommersant.ru/doc/7832307), [2](https://www.kommersant.ru/doc/6122817)), blog posts ([1](https://ifim.nus.edu.sg/tailor-made-2d-materials-became-closer-with-the-new-machine-learning-algorithm-developed-at-nus/), [2](https://communities.springernature.com/posts/sparse-representation-for-machine-learning-the-properties-of-defects-in-2d-materials)), a [science slam](https://elementy.ru/events/435506/IV_Nauchnye_boi_NIU_VShE_na_Dne_Vyshki), interviews ([1](https://web.archive.org/web/20210518043835/https://academy.yandex.ru/posts/beresh-i-razbiraeshsya), [2](https://habr.com/en/company/yandex/blog/422761/)), and a public [talk](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zLFZ_z-7-JI&feature=youtu.be&t=7779).

Taught at the Machine Learning in High Energy Physics Summer School in [2015](https://indico.cern.ch/event/439520/), [2016](https://indico.cern.ch/event/497368/), [2017](https://indico.cern.ch/event/613571/), [2018](https://indico.cern.ch/event/687473/), [2019](https://indico.cern.ch/event/768915/), [2020](https://indico.cern.ch/event/838377/), [2021](https://indico.cern.ch/event/1025052/), and [2023](https://indico.cern.ch/event/1229514/).
Lecturer in the [Coursera Advanced Machine Learning specialisation](https://www.coursera.org/specializations/aml), which is [award-winning](https://www.hse.ru/en/news/edu/259378493.html).
Taught and developed an introductory data analysis [course](https://we.hse.ru/en/) at HSE.

service.body: Reviewer for RSC Advances, Machine Learning: Science and Technology, and AI for Accelerated Materials Design workshops at NeurIPS and ICLR (2023–2025).
Peer Staff Supporter at NUS, serving as a first-line support for mental wellbeing.

otherItems.body: Aspiring stand-up comedian; bombed more than 20 times at open mics.
International Junior Science Olympiad 2008 in Korea, silver medal.
